reviews.review_title: I want more… and I know I shouldn’t
reviews.review_description: This is an adrenaline infused power trip beyond comprehension, that being said I only have a singular issue and that’s with the stand alone version, it crashes intermittently to my hub menu and as I converse with other streamers we all had the same issue, bar that I want more… more legs more enemies more upgrades more weapons, you guys have hit the nail on the head, please don’t stop working on this yet…. You can play with ya ball later, more marrow more marrow!!!

Update:

HEY META!!!  Why aren't you promoting games like this?! Are you blind?  Have you no fun left in your dark empty soul? This game should be getting tons of reviews by now, and it likely would have back when Oculus ran the show.

I do know Meta's not listening but I just had to yell anyway. They've abandoned the store and the good games struggle to be seen in a never ending flood of crap.

Meta's gotta start curating the store again like Oculus used to if they want the platform to thrive. Toss out the Gorilla Tag clones and other assorted trash that's burying the innovative games like this.

I'm tired of going dumpster diving through the store desperately looking for something that shouldn't have been tossed.

Original Review:

There's a bit of a learning curve but after spending a few minutes in it I was having a great time even though I have no skills yet.

Everything about it is kinda strange, funny, and disturbing all at the same time.

The retro graphics are wild and beautifully animated.

The sound effects match the madness of the visuals perfectly.

This is a tough game to describe, but if the trailer appeals to you don't think about it too much and just click the "purchase" button and be happy. 😁

This is one of the first VR games with a grappling-hook mechanic that I just immediately understood. The enemy types feeling a little samey after a while is more than made up for by the frantic action and fast movement, facilitated by small dashes, wall sliding, and a grappling hook that's so fun and intuitive that I forgot there's also a jump button.
More bonuses include left-handed control options just in time for the 1.0 release, leaderboards for fixed-seed runs, and decent performance in this native Quest version. 
So far my only real complaint at the time of writings is the default weapon only firing with trigger presses, which gets exhausting after a while. 
Marrow Marrow is an absolute delight to play in short bursts, with standard runs typically not lasting much more than half an hour.

If you’re looking for a lazy sit-down experience, look elsewhere.Marrow Marrow is the best high-octane, sweat-inducing Meta Quest masterpiece that finally proves what VR shooters are capable of when they stop trying to be "realistic" and start being pure fun.

- Relentless Blasting: Think Doom Eternal levels of aggression. The action is so intense that I was literally dripping sweat after just 30 minutes. It’s a full-body workout disguised as a top-tier arena shooter.
- Instant Action: No long cut-scenes. You’re in the thick of it immediately. It’s the perfect "quick VR-fix" when you want massive action right now.
- The Right Style & Atmosphere: The graphics are delightfully weird and creative. It has a distinct, trippy atmosphere that sets it apart from generic military shooters or overly cartoonish games. The enemy designs are super imaginative.
- Total Immersion: Marrow Marrow feels VR, isn’t just a flatscreen port as seen so often in VR today. The verticality is insane. Fluently jumping between skyscrapers and air-dashing like a maniac. You feel every bit of the height and speed.
- Fluid Movement: The parkour mechanics feel incredibly natural. It’s rare to find a game that handles high-speed movement this smoothly without making it feel clunky.
Bottom Line: If you have the "VR legs" for it, just buy this game. It’s a pure adrenaline shot, a visual trip, and easily one of the most responsive shooters on the Quest. My favorite discovery this year!

The core gameplay revolves around fluid movement — wall-running, grappling, dashing, and chaining attacks together while fighting waves of enemies. This mobility system is easily the highlight of the game. It feels smooth, responsive, and gives you a strong sense of control, especially once you get into the flow.

The game clearly takes inspiration from classic “boomer shooters,” focusing on speed and aggression rather than realism. Each run throws you into arena-style encounters with increasing difficulty, encouraging you to master both movement and weapon usage.  

You should play Marrow Marrow if you enjoy fast skill-based gameplay where movement matters as much as shooting. Arcade-style challenges that push you to improve and chase better run. A VR games that feel physical and intense, with lots of motion and flow.

reviews.review_title: Risk of Rain and ULTRAKILL had a baby
reviews.review_description: So if you'd like Risk of Rain and ULTRAKILL, in VR, this is a game for you! The movement is so good and the combat feels amazing. And don't get me started on the sound design, I absolutely adore it. The guns sound so satisfying. Also the visual style is very nice, simple, but cohesive. Overall, a super entertaining roguelike/boomer shooter with a lot of depth.
This game is Awesome! 🫠🤙 Marrow Marrow is quickly becoming my favorite app!  Just fantastic gameplay!  Let’s Go!!!

I don't normally write reviews but this game needed it. At the time of writing this there are only 14 other reviews.
This game is a simple movement based arena shooter, but it does everything it wants to perfectly. Intuitive controls, fast paced movement, and even a SHOULDER CANNON
Please give this game a try!

I was a little worried buying a game with only 3 reviews but I read a review on UPLOADVR and it sold me. Real happy I made the purchase.  I’m convinced at some point this game is gonna sell much better than right now. 

Reminds me a little of ZIX if you’ve played it.  The movement/grapple system is what sells this game for me.  I friggin love swinging all over the place taking down baddies. 

The weapons are varied so far (I’ve only unlocked 4-5 of em & tried 2 of em) and keeps me interested with all the unlockables. 

Only minor complaint is I wish there were some better/more interesting attributes to choose from after completing a level.  Maybe I simply haven’t gotten them yet but I’d love to see a chain powerup where you hit a guy and it hits anyone near him as well.  Or “15% of shots have chance to light enemy on fire”.  I can’t be certain what is and isn’t offered yet though so don’t quote me just yet. 

Really hope to see the dev stick with this game and update in the future. Would LOVE to see more weapons added.
